Finding: the current failure is not only coordinate drift; it is hierarchy confusion. Hair is a parent occlusion region with a face cutout; broad skin is a face+neck parent that must be split; jaw/chin and neck are separate; lips and teeth are separate, with teeth only the thin visible slit; eyes, irises, pupils, upper eyelids, lower eyelids, brows, and under-eye regions are nested child regions. These references are not pixel-exact active masks and did not promote any row.

No row can be called accepted, candidate-passed, locally passed, done, complete, generalized, certification-ready, or target-runtime-ready unless it cites explicit `wave70_mask_promotion_gate_pass == true` evidence and the distinct approval token `W70_MASK_PROMOTION_ROW_GATE_PASS_TRUE` for that exact row. Conditional wording, generated-output stability, or a prior boolean field is not approval.

Required next work is not another runtime proof. Required next work is to repair the mask-generation/review method and create per-mask hard-gate evidence with large source crop, mask-only crop, source+mask overlay, protected-boundary overlay, target definition, protected-neighbor matrix, user-dispute check, and generated-output stability kept separate. Edge-only or contour-only masks must be labeled as such; they cannot pass as full anatomical masks.

## Wave70 Canonical Protected Boundary Registry Required - 2026-07-07T20:37:00-05:00

User asked how masks are prevented from crossing protected boundaries and whether a bad `mf70_nose` mask crossing into the mouth could corrupt later `mf70_mouth_lips` creation. Decision: a generated editable mask is not a canonical protected boundary by default. If the nose mask is wrong, the mouth mask must not inherit that wrong nose region. Both masks must be validated against a source-derived canonical boundary registry and protected-overlap matrix for the current source image/matrix slot.

New protocol `Plan/Instructions/QA/WAVE70_PROTECTED_BOUNDARY_REGISTRY_PROTOCOL.md`, SHA256 `f5345320deedcff75fd74b0d29f36d704a6a1a137f5f80cc5f3eaa0ec8c4ef05`, is now required alongside mask alignment and reference-matrix QA. Enforcement evidence is `Plan/Instructions/QA/Evidence/Mask_Factory/Wave70/W70_PROTECTED_BOUNDARY_REGISTRY_ENFORCEMENT_20260707T203700-0500.json`, SHA256 `26c9e88867c23b2f9282ce438622cbf8490c775e0e936b5c48687d1e25e5342a`, with tracker evidence `Plan/Tracker/Evidence/W70_PROTECTED_BOUNDARY_REGISTRY_ENFORCEMENT_20260707T203700-0500.json`, SHA256 `e559faf32fc117d593d8bbc463355432cf780a337c211def035f1e54af9eb2c5`.

Tracker and item gates now require `canonical_protected_boundary_registry_pass`, `protected_overlap_matrix_pass`, `boundary_registry_manifest_present`, and `protected_boundary_noninheritance_pass`. Current script observation: some mask scripts have rough manual exclusion boxes, while others rely on hand-tuned polygons and QA notes. Generalized future work must replace that with source-adaptive canonical boundary layers and overlap checks before any local pass, generalized pass, or certification-ready claim.

## Wave70 Semantic Mask-Alignment QA Gate Enforced - 2026-07-07T19:05:00-05:00

Wave70 mask QA has been tightened immediately. New protocol `Plan/Instructions/QA/WAVE70_MASK_ALIGNMENT_QA_PROTOCOL.md` is now required for Wave70 mask rows: semantic mask alignment, protected-neighbor review, and generated-output stability are separate gates. A stable low-denoise generated output no longer implies the mask is semantically correct.

Retro audit evidence is `Plan/Instructions/QA/Evidence/Mask_Factory/Wave70/W70_MASK_ALIGNMENT_RETRO_AUDIT_20260707T184000-0500.json`, SHA256 `19163f1d975aa90b05fa5a46148139c17e126dffef835508ef944973dc715fe7`, with tracker evidence `Plan/Tracker/Evidence/W70_MASK_ALIGNMENT_RETRO_AUDIT_20260707T184000-0500.json`, SHA256 `d9abce07be986f3e5156ae8293ef349b39ceb5e4565fbe1ad47be9c0893e237d`. Existing generated-output-safe rows were reclassified: `mf70_expression_region`, `mf70_cheeks_skin`, `mf70_jawline_chin`, `mf70_skin_tone_continuity`, `mf70_left_eye`, `mf70_right_eye`, and `mf70_eyelids` now need mask-alignment revision; `mf70_pupils_iris_sclera` is mask-alignment failed; `mf70_eyes_full` is generated-output stable but mask-alignment unreviewed; `mf70_forehead_skin`, `mf70_eyelashes`, `mf70_under_eye`, and `mf70_eyebrows` are mask-alignment pass with notes while still target-runtime pending.

All Wave70 tracker and item rows now include row-level gate requirements for `semantic_mask_alignment_pass`, `protected_neighbor_check_pass`, and `generated_output_safe_pass`; evidence requirements now include semantic mask-alignment review. `mf70_eyebrows` / `TRK-W70-0016` / `ITEM-W70-0016` was updated from the old generated-output-only status to `Mask_Alignment_Pass_Generated_Output_Safe_Target_Runtime_Pending`. Updated mask QA is `Plan/Instructions/QA/Evidence/Mask_Factory/Wave70/mf70_eyebrows.json`, SHA256 `684da3d4f0d3286abbfee8d2a21a57760e401aae72b48ccbc4f410cf6d65b1f6`; updated tracker evidence is `Plan/Tracker/Evidence/W70_MF70_EYEBROWS_GENERATED_OUTPUT_20260707T190000-0500.json`, SHA256 `9bd19c497bf3496ee0fc34a84291aa60c5b0a623a3a1a3ba370e40dc96aae8b2`.

No scheduled cron job was changed; no project cron/scheduled task was found that directly enforces Wave70 mask QA. Enforcement is now in the Plan, Instructions, Tracker, Items, QA protocol, and evidence files. Next Wave70 local work must not use `Local_Generated_Output_Proof_Pass_Target_Runtime_Pending`; use the stricter mask-alignment status vocabulary instead.

## Local Hand/Contact Visual Certification Gate Added And Blocks Final Certification - 2026-07-07T12:35:00-05:00

The Wave25 two-character hand-to-body contact now has a stricter local hand/contact visual certification gate. No EC2, AWS, GitHub API, Civitai, S3, Wave65, broad validator, or checkpointing activity was used.

New helper `Plan/Instructions/QA/Scripts/Test-HandContactVisualCertification.ps1` consumes a certification request, source requirements, generated images, contact-mask QA evidence, robustness visual QA evidence, and visual review findings. Request `runtime_artifacts/multi_character_interaction/two_character_hand_to_body_w69/HAND_CONTACT_VISUAL_CERTIFICATION_REQUEST.json` checked the current seed `210701`, `210702`, and `210703` outputs against Wave20 hand/finger QA, Wave22 source/target binding, Wave23 contact-to-deformation requirements, Wave25 cross-character contact/occlusion rules, and Wave13 contact-mask tests.

## Next Exact Work
First, refresh AWS login/SSO for expected account `029530099913`, then rerun `Test-AwsAuthGate.ps1`, `Test-AwsProfileAuthMatrix.ps1`, and `Test-LaneRuntimeReadiness.ps1` for `sdxl_realvisxl_controlnet_canny_lane`. Do not start EC2 unless the auth gate reports `safe_to_start_ec2=true`.

Second, create/verify a fresh emergency stop schedule for the static-proof window, then run `Invoke-EC2LaneStaticProof.ps1` for `sdxl_realvisxl_controlnet_canny_lane` from a clean pushed head. Rerun lane readiness after static proof.

Third, run one bounded EC2 workflow smoke from `runtime_artifacts\run_packages\sdxl_realvisxl_controlnet_canny_lane_static_package_v1\RUN_PACKAGE_MANIFEST.json` only if static proof and readiness pass. Pull back artifacts, run technical image QA and whole-image visual QA, update tracker/evidence/hydration files, and certify only the lane result that actually passed.

Fourth, if broader image-quality certification becomes the explicit target instead, create a new multi-sample QA plan rather than rerunning already certified RealVisXL matrix samples.

Keep using the cost-control lane before any future generation attempt:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\tools\Test-LocalComfyUIDevPreflight.ps1 -ProjectRoot C:\Comfy_UI_Main -LaneId sdxl_realvisxl_base_lane
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\tools\Start-LocalComfyUIDev.ps1 -ProjectRoot C:\Comfy_UI_Main -LocalComfyRoot <path-to-local-ComfyUI> -LowVram
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\tools\New-WorkflowRunPackage.ps1 -ProjectRoot C:\Comfy_UI_Main -LaneId sdxl_realvisxl_base_lane -AllowNonFirstLane
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\tools\New-EC2DeployBundle.ps1 -ProjectRoot C:\Comfy_UI_Main -LaneId sdxl_realvisxl_base_lane -RunPackageManifestFile <realvisxl-run-package-manifest>
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\Plan\Instructions\Operations\Scripts\Publish-DeployBundleToS3.ps1 -BundleManifestFile <deploy-bundle-manifest> -S3BaseUri s3://<bucket>/<deploy-bundle-prefix>
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\Plan\Instructions\Operations\Scripts\New-EC2EmergencyStopSchedule.ps1 -SchedulerRoleArn arn:aws:iam::<account-id>:role/<scheduler-stop-role> -StopAfterMinutes 60
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Comfy_UI_Main\Plan\Instructions\Operations\Scripts\Start-EC2InstanceStopWatchdog.ps1 -StopAfterMinutes 60
```

Do not rerun RealVisXL static proof, workflow smoke, pullback, or image QA for the completed proof unless the lane, prompt, model, runtime, or QA objective changed. The next runtime evidence should belong to a new lane/module or a user-approved broader quality pass.

## Hard Stop And No-Loop Rules
If AWS auth is expired, Git is not clean/pushed, or the selected lane is not ready, do not start EC2. Record the blocker once and switch only to a concrete local/CI task that changes runtime capability.

Allowed local/CI tasks while EC2 is stopped:

- Improve a lane, prompt profile, model registry record, run package, deploy bundle, or QA rule.
- Run local ComfyUI dev checks/previews without claiming EC2 equivalence.
- Run GitHub Actions preflight/package and inspect the artifact.
- Fix a real stale/conflicting instruction that would misroute the autonomous session.

Disallowed loop work:

- Repeating first-lane proof without a changed lane/package/prompt/QA objective.
- Rebuilding indexes or handoffs repeatedly with the same result.
- Running Git LFS pulls on the EC2 clock unless a lane explicitly needs them.
- Starting EC2 to upload/sync project files when a deploy bundle can be prepared and uploaded to S3 first.
- Starting EC2 for prompt/workflow iteration that local ComfyUI can handle.
- Updating this pursuing goal in a way that omits the required `Plan/Instructions` read order.

## Update Protocol
When this file is autonomously updated, preserve these sections and keep the required instruction read order. Updates should change only current status, last verified facts, next exact work, and hard blockers. Do not compress this file back into a short goal that omits `Plan/Instructions`.
